title: Runge-Kutta 4 Method Explorer
summary: Interactive exploration of the Runge-Kutta 4 method for solving ordinary differential equations with theory, examples, and live calculator.
search_plain: Runge-Kutta 4 Method Explorer | Joshua Coetzer Blog Portfolio Blog Mathematics & Programming 2024-12-01 Runge-Kutta 4 Method Explorer Interactive Numerical Solution for Ordinary Differential Equations Theory Behind RK4 What is the Runge-Kutta 4 Method? The Runge-Kutta 4 (RK4) method is a widely used numerical technique for solving ordinary differential equations (ODEs). It provides a balance between computational efficiency and accuracy, making it one of the most popular choices for numerical integration. General form of first-order ODE: \[ \frac{dy}{dt} = f(t, y), \quad y(t_0) = y_0 \] RK4 Algorithm The RK4 method approximates the solution by calculating four intermediate slopes (k₁ to k₄) and combining them: Slope calculations: \[ k_1 = f(t_n, y_n) \] \[ k_2 = f\left(t_n + \frac{h}{2}, y_n + \frac{h}{2}k_1\right) \] \[ k_3 = f\left(t_n + \frac{h}{2}, y_n + \frac{h}{2}k_2\right) \] \[ k_4 = f(t_n + h, y_n + h k_3) \] Next value: \[ y_{n+1} = y_n + \frac{h}{6}(k_1 + 2k_2 + 2k_3 + k_4) \] Where: \( h \) = step size \( t_{n+1} = t_n + h \) Visualization Why RK4? The RK4 method offers several advantages over simpler methods like Euler's method: Higher Accuracy: Fourth-order accuracy means errors decrease as h⁴ Stability: More stable than lower-order methods for larger step sizes Versatility: Works well for a wide range of differential equations Balance: Good compromise between computational cost and accuracy RK4 Calculator Select ODE Example: Exponential Growth: dy/dt = y Logistic Growth: dy/dt = y(1 - y) Harmonic Oscillator: dy/dt = -x, dx/dt = y Custom ODE Custom ODE (JavaScript syntax): function(t, y) { return y; } Use 't' for time and 'y' for the dependent variable. Initial Value (y₀): Step Size (h): 0.1 Start Time (t₀): End Time: Calculate RK4 Solution Results Step Time (t) y(t) Applications and Extensions Real-World Applications Population dynamics modeling Chemical reaction kinetics Orbital mechanics and celestial body motion Electrical circuit analysis Fluid dynamics simulations Economic growth models Advanced Topics Adaptive step size methods Higher-order Runge-Kutta methods Implicit Runge-Kutta methods Systems of differential equations Stiff differential equations Error estimation and control ← Back to Blog © 2020 TarsOnlineCafe. All rights reserved. The RK4 method was developed by German mathematicians Carl Runge and Martin Kutta around 1900.

title: Deep Dive: Job Search Agent AI Project
summary: A detailed breakdown of the Job Search Agent AI project: architecture, code, and how it automates job hunting.
search_plain: Deep Dive: Job Search Agent AI Project | Joshua Coetzer Blog AI News & Projects 2024-06-10 Deep Dive: Job Search Agent AI Project Job Search Agent is an open-source AI-powered tool that automates the process of searching and applying for jobs. Let's break down its architecture, core logic, and see how you can use or extend it for your own job-hunting automation. Project Overview Automates job search and application using AI agents Uses LangChain , OpenAI , and Streamlit for the interface Supports resume parsing, job scraping, and personalized cover letter generation 1. Installation & Setup Clone the repo and install dependencies: git clone https://github.com/surapuramakhil-org/Job_search_agent.git cd Job_search_agent pip install -r requirements.txt Set your OpenAI API key in your environment: export OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key-here 2. Core Architecture The project is modular, with agents for scraping, parsing, and applying. The main entrypoint is main.py : # main.py import streamlit as st from src.job_search_agent import JobSearchAgent def main(): st.title("Job Search Agent") agent = JobSearchAgent() # ...UI and logic... if __name__ == "__main__": main() 3. The JobSearchAgent Class This class orchestrates the workflow: scraping jobs, parsing resumes, and applying. # src/job_search_agent.py class JobSearchAgent: def __init__(self): # Initialize models, tools, etc. pass def search_jobs(self, query): # Scrape jobs from sources pass def parse_resume(self, resume_path): # Extract info from resume pass def generate_cover_letter(self, job, resume_info): # Use LLM to generate cover letter pass def apply(self, job, cover_letter): # Submit application pass 4. Scraping & Parsing Job scraping is handled by job_scraper.py : # src/job_scraper.py def scrape_jobs(query): # Use requests/BeautifulSoup to scrape job boards return jobs_list Resume parsing uses pdfminer or similar libraries: # src/resume_parser.py def parse_resume(resume_path): # Extract text and info from PDF return resume_info 5. AI-Powered Cover Letter Generation The agent uses LangChain and OpenAI's GPT models to generate tailored cover letters: # src/cover_letter_generator.py from langchain.llms import OpenAI def generate_cover_letter(job, resume_info): prompt = f"Write a cover letter for {job['title']} using my resume: {resume_info}" llm = OpenAI() return llm(prompt) 6. Streamlit UI The Streamlit app provides a simple interface for uploading your resume, entering job search queries, and tracking applications. # main.py (UI snippet) st.file_uploader("Upload your resume") st.text_input("Job search query") if st.button("Search & Apply"): # Run agent 7. Extending the Project Add more job boards by extending job_scraper.py Improve resume parsing with NLP Integrate with LinkedIn or other APIs Enhance UI for better tracking Conclusion Job Search Agent is a great foundation for automating job applications using AI. Check out the GitHub repo for more details and contribute! ← Previous All Posts Next →
